Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to analyze an event log of a customer support ticket resolution process I need to Identify cases with significantly longer total resolution times Determine potential root causes of these delays escalations wait times etc Explain how these factors lead to increased cycle times and propose insights recommendations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets calculate the duration for each case Case 101 2024 03 01 08 00 to 2024 03 01 10 15 2 hours 15 minutes Case 102 2024 03 01 08 05 to 2024 03 02 09 15 25 hours 10 minutes Case 103 2024 03 01 08 10 to 2024 03 01 09 30 1 hour 20 minutes Case 104 2024 03 01 08 20 to 2024 03 02 08 30 24 hours 10 minutes Case 105 2024 03 01 08 25 to 2024 03 03 09 30 49 hours 5 minutes

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Cases with significantly longer resolution times 102 104 and especially 105

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets look at the activities and timestamps for these long cases Case 102 08 05 Receive 08 30 Triage 25m wait 09 00 Assign 30m wait 11 30 Escalate to L2 2h30m wait 14 00 Investigate 2h30m wait Next day 09 00 Resolve 19h wait overnight 09 15 Close Case 104 08 20 Receive 09 00 Triage 40m wait 09 30 Assign 30m wait 13 00 Investigate 3h30m wait Next day 08 00 Resolve 19h wait overnight 08 30 Close Case 105 08 25 Receive 08 35 Triage 10m wait 09 00 Assign 25m wait 09 10 Investigate 10m wait 10 00 Escalate to L2 50m wait Next day 14 00 Investigate 28h wait overnight Next day 09 00 Resolve 19h wait overnight 09 30 Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Root causes Escalations to Level 2 Agents Cases 102 and 105 involved escalations Case 102 waited 2 5 hours after escalation for investigation Case 105 waited 28 hours for Level 2 investigation Long waiting times between investigation and resolution In all long cases 102 104 105 there is a significant delay between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket often spanning overnight For example Case 104 has no escalation but takes over 19 hours from investigation to resolution Case 102 goes from 14 00 to 09 00 the next day Case 105 goes from 14 00 to 09 00 the next day Delays before investigation Case 104 has a 3 5 hour delay between Assign to Level 1 Agent and Investigate Issue

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Escalations create bottlenecks particularly if Level 2 agents are overwhelmed or have high response times The step from Investigate Issue to Resolve Ticket often crosses into the next day indicating a potential lack of resources staffing in the afternoon evening or that investigations are complex and get deferred to the next day Recommendations Improve Level 2 staffing or routing Monitor active cases late in the day Establish SLAs for time to investigation post escalation Provide better triage to assign directly to L2 if L1 cannot handle it
